layouts.name: Feinschliff · Title · Accent
layouts.when_to_use: Deck cover, chapter opener, or a strong single-statement slide where the headline IS the content. The headline should be a complete phrase under 8 words.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Any slide with supporting detail, numbers, or imagery. For those, prefer 'title-picture', 'chapter-orange', or a data layout.

eyebrow.description: Mono uppercase label above the title

title.description: 2-line display headline — use \n for line break

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Title · Ink
layouts.when_to_use: Same as title-orange but on dark ink background — use for serious/formal contexts (earnings, safety, compliance).
layouts.when_not_to_use: Light-hearted topics. Any slide with substantial detail.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Title + Picture
layouts.when_to_use: Product-led openers, section intros where a single hero image reinforces the title. The right-half image frame is a fixed shape — users replace it via right-click → Format Shape → Fill → Picture.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Data-heavy slides or when multiple visuals/columns are required — prefer kpi-grid, text-picture, or a column layout instead.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Agenda
layouts.when_to_use: Deck agenda with 3-6 items. Each item has a short title + one-line description.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Fewer than 3 items (use title-orange) or more than 6 (use 3-column or 4-column).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Chapter · Accent
layouts.when_to_use: Between major sections. Shows chapter number + title. Bold divider slide.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Only if the deck has one section — then skip the chapter opener entirely.

title.description: Format '01\nBrand.' — number + topic on two lines

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Chapter · Ink + Picture
layouts.when_to_use: Chapter opener for serious/formal sections; includes a right-half editorial image frame. Users replace the image via right-click → Format Shape → Fill → Picture.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Light/celebratory sections — use chapter-orange instead.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · KPI Grid
layouts.when_to_use: 2-4 high-level quantitative figures, each with unit + label + delta. Most scannable format for executive updates.
layouts.when_not_to_use: More than 4 figures (use a table or bar chart). Figures without clear units or time-comparisons.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · 2-Column Cards
layouts.when_to_use: Two parallel concepts of equal weight — comparison, pair of principles, two-track plan.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Three or more items (use 3-column or 4-column). Content-heavy analysis (use text-picture).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · 3-Column
layouts.when_to_use: Three parallel items — pillars of a strategy, three brands/products, three phases.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Two items (use 2-column-cards) or four+ (use 4-column-cards).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · 4-Column Cards
layouts.when_to_use: Four sequential or parallel items — classic Q1-Q4 plan, 4-phase roadmap.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Fewer than 4 or more than 4 items. Plan with items of unequal weight.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Text + Picture
layouts.when_to_use: One substantive idea explained with a supporting visual. Has buttons for CTA. Users replace the image via right-click → Format Shape → Fill → Picture.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Pure text slide (use 2-column-cards). Pure image (use full-bleed-cover).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Full-bleed Cover
layouts.when_to_use: Strong image + short statement. Chapter opener or deck cover with high visual impact. The full-bleed image area is a fixed shape — users replace it via right-click → Format Shape → Fill → Picture.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Content-led slides where text carries the meaning. Prefer a title or content layout.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Bar Chart
layouts.when_to_use: 2-6 items with quantitative values to compare, e.g. revenue by region, survey responses.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Continuous time-series (use a line chart — not yet in Baukasten). More than 6 items (use a table).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Components Showcase
layouts.when_to_use: Internal design-system reference — showing available buttons, chips, rules.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Regular content — this slide is brand-authoring reference, not for end-user decks.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Quote
layouts.when_to_use: Strong voice moment — customer testimonial, leadership principle, brand tenet.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Body text pretending to be a quote. Must be an actual quotation.

title.description: The quote itself, including opening + closing quotation marks

layouts.name: Feinschliff · End
layouts.when_to_use: Final slide of the deck. Always include for deck closure.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Never — every deck ends with this or a similar closer.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Graphical
layouts.when_to_use: 3–6 category values that share a scale (e.g. regional share of revenue).
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for time-series — use mck-stacked-bar or mck-waterfall. Avoid for >6 categories — use mck-table.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Action Title
layouts.when_to_use: Any analytical slide where the title itself should carry the takeaway (McKinsey-style “so what?”). Ideal when the slide's one job is to land a decision or claim.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for descriptive or reference content; use title-with-visual or content-columns instead.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Horizontal Bullets
layouts.when_to_use: Three parallel drivers / forces / buckets, each backed by 2–4 short bullets. Good for context or situation slides.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid when one column deserves more weight; use content-with-visual. Avoid for time-ordered content; use data-timeline.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Vertical Bullets
layouts.when_to_use: Prose lede on the left plus a numbered list of 3–6 moves on the right. Good for “we will do X, Y, Z.”
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for comparisons of equal items; use content-columns. Avoid for time-boxed sequences; use mck-process-flow.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · 2×2 Matrix
layouts.when_to_use: Classic 2×2 prioritisation grid with one focus quadrant flagged. Good for portfolio choices.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid if axes can't be named with one crisp word each; use content-columns instead.

cells.description: Cells in reading order: TL, TR, BL, BR.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Waterfall
layouts.when_to_use: Financial or metric bridge between two anchor totals across 3–7 positive/negative drivers.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for non-additive metrics (ratios, averages). Avoid if driver count >7; use mck-table instead.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Process Flow
layouts.when_to_use: 3–6 sequential stages with chevron arrows; one stage can be highlighted as current. Good for delivery rhythms.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for non-sequential buckets; use content-columns. Avoid if stages don't flow into each other.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Stacked Bar
layouts.when_to_use: Multi-year or multi-category revenue/share mix across 3–6 time buckets and 2–5 segments.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid if the story is about a single segment — use a line chart. Avoid if segment count >5 — the bars become illegible.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Table
layouts.when_to_use: 5+ rows of comparable metrics (product lines, regions, teams) with 3–6 columns of numbers and variances.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for 2–3 row comparisons — use content-columns. Avoid when a chart would show the pattern faster.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Pyramid
layouts.when_to_use: 3-tier value hierarchy (foundation → trust → meaning) shown as stacked triangular tiers with a side key.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for more than 4 tiers — becomes illegible. Avoid for non-hierarchical relationships; use content-columns.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Key Takeaways
layouts.when_to_use: End-of-section summary of 3 most important points, each with an owner.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for 1–2 points (use a quote or title-cover). Avoid for >4 points (the card shrinks and loses impact).

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Executive Summary
layouts.when_to_use: One-page summary of 3–5 moves, each with status commentary and a headline metric. Ideal for board or exec readouts.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for narrative chapters; use content-columns instead. Avoid if the moves don't each have a target metric.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Line Chart
layouts.when_to_use: Two-to-three metric trend over 3–8 time buckets (years or quarters). Good for showing divergence or trajectory.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for single-point comparisons — use KPIs. Avoid for >3 series — becomes tangled; use mck-stacked-bar or mck-table.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Scorecard
layouts.when_to_use: Compare 3–5 options against 4–6 criteria using Harvey balls. Use when the recommendation needs defensible, visible trade-offs.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for quantitative comparisons where the numbers themselves matter; use mck-table. Avoid for 2-option decisions; use content-columns.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Gantt
layouts.when_to_use: Show 3–6 parallel workstreams over 3–6 time buckets with dependencies and milestones. Good for programme reviews.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for conceptual (non-dated) plans — use mck-process-flow. Avoid for a single-workstream timeline — use data-timeline cards.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Funnel
layouts.when_to_use: Multi-stage conversion view (acquisition, activation, retention) with volume and rate at each step.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for time-series — use mck-line-chart. Avoid if stages aren't a strict sequence — use mck-stacked-bar.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · Venn
layouts.when_to_use: Show the strategic sweet spot where 2–3 capabilities overlap. Good for positioning and differentiation slides.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for quantitative overlap — a Venn is illustrative, not proportional. Avoid for >3 sets — becomes illegible; use mck-pyramid.

layouts.name: Feinschliff · V-Model
layouts.when_to_use: Show the software-engineering V-model: paired verification phases (left, top-down) and validation phases (right, bottom-up) converging at implementation. Great when the narrative is 'every design step has a matching test'.
layouts.when_not_to_use: Avoid for agile or iterative timelines — use process-flow or gantt. Avoid if the audience has no engineering context. Avoid for 2-phase or 6+-phase processes (the tapered grid is tuned to 4 pairs).

kicker.description: Mono orange uppercase label above the title

pairs.description: Four phase/test pairs, top to bottom (broadest to narrowest).

pivot_counter.description: Small label above the pivot title, e.g. 'Pivot'

pivot_title.description: Centre label for the pivot block, e.g. 'Coding & implementation'
